title: Mock Exam F
description: A 22-task RHCSA9 mock exam covering persistent networking, repositories, users, services, storage, NFS, SSH, and rootless containers across client and server.

tasks: On client, recover root access from the console and set the root password to cinder9.

On client, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.45/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: client-f.exam9.lab
On client, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On client, install lsof from the configured repositories and remove tcpdump if it is installed.
On client, create group opsf9. Create users anaf9, devf9, and auditf9; auditf9 must use /sbin/nologin. Set each password to cinder9 and add anaf9 and devf9 to opsf9.
On client, set maximum password age 60 days and warning period 7 days for anaf9. Allow members of opsf9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On client, create /srv/opsf9 owned by root:opsf9 with permissions 2770 and a default ACL granting opsf9 full access.
On client, create executable script /usr/local/bin/report-f9 that writes the active state of sshd, chronyd, and firewalld to /root/report-f9.txt.
On client, create a 512 MiB swap file at /swapf9, activate it immediately, and make it persistent.
On client, create volume group vgf9 on /dev/sdb, create logical volume dataf9 with size 320 MiB, format it as XFS, and mount it persistently at /mnt/dataf9.
On client, create user podf9, enable lingering for that user, and run a rootless container named webf9 from localhost/rhcsa-httpd-base:latest.

On server, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.3/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: server-f.exam9.lab
On server, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On server, create group srvf9, create user svcf9 with password cinder9, add svcf9 to srvf9, and allow srvf9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On server, publish /var/www/html/exam-f.html containing RHCSA9-F, configure httpd to listen on TCP port 8305, label the port for httpd, and open it permanently in firewalld.
On server, enable persistent systemd journal storage and restart systemd-journald.
On server, create and enable auditf9.timer so it runs every 10 minutes and appends server-f to /var/log/auditf9.log.
On server, set the default boot target to multi-user.target and create /srv/server-f9 owned by root:srvf9 with permissions 2770.
On server, export /exports/rhcsa9-f to 192.168.122.0/24. On client, mount server:/exports/rhcsa9-f persistently at /mnt/rhcsa9-f.
On server, create user copyf9 with password cinder9. On client, configure key-based SSH login for root to copyf9@server.
On client, create /root/exam-f-copy.txt containing RHCSA9-F and copy it to server:/home/copyf9/exam-f-copy.txt.
On server, enable chronyd for the lab network. On client, configure chronyd to use server as its only time source.
solution_commands: # At the boot menu, edit the selected kernel entry.
# Append rw init=/bin/bash to the linux line and boot with Ctrl+x.
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.45/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
for service in sshd chronyd firewalld; do
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.3/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
checks: echo cinder9 | su - root -c 'whoami' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx root

hostnamectl --static | grep -qx client-f.exam9.lab && CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.45/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3

test -f /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '^baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/?$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '^baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/?$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && [ "$(grep -Ec '^enabled=1$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ] && [ "$(grep -Ec '^gpgcheck=0$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ]
rpm -q lsof >/dev/null

getent group opsf9 >/dev/null && id -nG anaf9 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsf9 && id -nG devf9 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsf9 && getent passwd auditf9 | awk -F: '$7 == "/sbin/nologin" {found=1} END {exit !found}'

chage -l anaf9 | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*60' && chage -l anaf9 | grep -Eq 'warning.*7' && grep -Eq '^%opsf9[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$' /etc/sudoers.d/opsf9-systemctl
stat -c '%U:%G:%a' /srv/opsf9 | grep -qx root:opsf9:2770 && getfacl -p /srv/opsf9 | grep -Eq '^default:group:opsf9:rwx$'
test -x /usr/local/bin/report-f9 && /usr/local/bin/report-f9 >/dev/null && test -s /root/report-f9.txt
swapon --show=NAME --noheadings | grep -qx '/swapf9' && awk '$1 == "/swapf9" && $2 == "swap" && $3 == "swap" {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
lvs /dev/vgf9/dataf9 >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET /mnt/dataf9 | grep -qx /mnt/dataf9 && awk '$2 == "/mnt/dataf9" && $3 == "xfs" {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
runuser -l podf9 -c 'podman ps --format {{.Names}}' | grep -qx webf9 && loginctl show-user podf9 | grep -Eq '^Linger=yes$'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; hostnamectl --static | grep -qx server-f.exam9.lab && CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.3/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; test -f /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^enabled=1$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ] && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^gpgcheck=0$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ]'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group srvf9 >/dev/null && id -nG svcf9 | tr '"'"' '"'"' '"'"'\n'"'"' | grep -qx srvf9 && grep -Eq '"'"'^%srvf9[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$'"'"' /etc/sudoers.d/srvf9-systemctl'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq RHCSA9-F /var/www/html/exam-f.html && grep -Eq '"'"'^Listen[[:space:]]+8305$'"'"' /etc/httpd/conf.d/exam-f.conf && semanage port -l | awk '"'"'$1 == "http_port_t" && $2 == "tcp" && $0 ~ /(^|[ ,])8305([, ]|$)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' && firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8305/tcp && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '"'"'/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf'"'"' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk '"'"'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' $files'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled auditf9.timer | grep -qx enabled && grep -Eq '"'"'^OnCalendar=\*:0/10$'"'"' /etc/systemd/system/auditf9.timer && grep -Fxq '"'"'echo server-f >> /var/log/auditf9.log'"'"' /usr/local/sbin/auditf9.sh'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl get-default | grep -qx multi-user.target && stat -c '"'"'%U:%G:%a'"'"' /srv/server-f9 | grep -qx root:srvf9:2770'

findmnt -no SOURCE,TARGET /mnt/rhcsa9-f | grep -qx 'server:/exports/rhcsa9-f /mnt/rhcsa9-f' && grep -Eq '^server:/exports/rhcsa9-f[[:space:]]+/mnt/rhcsa9-f[[:space:]]+nfs([[:space:]]|$)' /etc/fstab && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Eq "^/exports/rhcsa9-f[[:space:]]+192\\.168\\.122\\.0/24" /etc/exports.d/rhcsa9-f.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active'
runuser -l root -c 'ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o NumberOfPasswordPrompts=0 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null copyf9@server true'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq RHCSA9-F /home/copyf9/exam-f-copy.txt'

grep -Eq '^server[[:space:]]+server[[:space:]]+iburst$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active chronyd | grep -qx active'
